zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Alexander MacDonald 2nd of Lochalsh於大約1448年出生在英國蘇格蘭可洛馬迪夏爾時，他的父親Celestine MacDonald 1st of Lochalsh20歲，母親Finola Maclean of Duart20歲。他於大約1469年與Lady MacDonald Lochalsh在英國蘇格蘭可洛馬迪夏爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1494年登記服兵役。他去世於1494年10月14日在蘇格蘭阿吉爾享年47歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾。

nameMeanings.name: MacDonald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: McDonald
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Domhnaill ‘son of Domhnall’, a name derived from the Celtic elements domno- ‘world’ + val- ‘might, rule’. Donald is an Anglicized form (via Latin) of this personal name used in Scotland, though the surname is also widespread in Ireland. The name is equivalent to Irish McDonnell and McConnell , and to Manx Cannell .

History: This is the name of the largest and most disparate of the Scottish clans (Clan Donald), associated in particular with the Hebrides and claiming descent from Domhnall mac Raghnaill mac Somhairle, who lived in the late 12th century. From that time until 1493 the head of the clan was known as Lord (or King) of the Isles. The reigns of the Lords of the Isles were always stormy, often in conflict with the kings of Scotland, and peppered with disasters. After a series of defeats in the 1480s, Eoin Mac Dhomhnaill a Ìle (John Macdonald of Islay, Earl of Ross and Lord of the Isles) forfeited his lands, his power, and his title as Lord of the Isles in 1493 to King James IV of Scotland. By then, a branch of the family had settled in the Antrim Glens in Ireland and members moved between the southern Hebrides and Ireland throughout the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Alexander MacDonald 2nd of Lochalsh was born about 1448, in Cromartyshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Celestine MacDonald 1st of Lochalsh, was 20 and his mother, Finola Maclean of Duart, was 20. He married Lady MacDonald Lochalsh about 1469, in Cromartyshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He registered for military service in 1494. He died on 14 October 1494, in Argyll, Scotland, at the age of 47, and was buried in Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Alexander MacDonald 2nd of Lochalsh est né environ 1448, à Cromartyshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Celestine MacDonald 1st of Lochalsh, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Finola Maclean of Duart, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Lady MacDonald Lochalsh environ 1469, à Cromartyshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1494. Il est décédé le 14 octobre 1494, à Argyll, Écosse, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Argyll, Écosse.

Er hat um 1469 in Cromartyshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Lady MacDonald Lochalsh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er hat sich 1494 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 14. ja.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander MacDonald 2nd of Lochalshが約1448年にイギリススコットランドクロマルトイシュアイアで生まれた時，父Celestine MacDonald 1st of Lochalshは20歳，母Finola Maclean of Duartは20歳であった。約1469年にイギリススコットランドクロマルトイシュアイアでLady MacDonald Lochalshと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1494年に兵役登録をした。彼は1494年10月14日にスコットランドアルグイルで47歳で死去し，スコットランドアルグイルにて埋葬された。

ko.Tulloch Castle, Dingwall, Ross & Cromart: 영국 스코틀랜드 로스 앤드 크로마티 딩월
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander MacDonald 2nd of Lochalsh(이)가 대략 1448년 영국 스코틀랜드 크로마티실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Celestine MacDonald 1st of Lochalsh(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Finola Maclean of Duart(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1469년 영국 스코틀랜드 크로마티실르에서 Lady MacDonald Lochalsh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1494년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1494년 10월 14일 스컷랜드 아그이에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Alexander MacDonald 2nd of Lochalsh nasceu aproximadamente 1448, em Cromartyshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Celestine MacDonald 1st of Lochalsh, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Finola Maclean of Duart, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady MacDonald Lochalsh aproximadamente 1469, em Cromartyshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1494. Ele faleceu em 14 de outubro de 1494, em Argyll, Scotland, com 47 anos, e foi sepultado em Argyll, Scotland.

ru.Tulloch Castle, Dingwall, Ross & Cromart: дингвалл, росс анд кромерти, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Alexander MacDonald 2nd of Lochalsh родился около 1448 г. в местности кромертишир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Celestine MacDonald 1st of Lochalsh было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Finola Maclean of Duart, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady MacDonald Lochalsh около 1469 г. в местности кромертишир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1494 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 14 октября 1494 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности аргилл, Шотландия.
